description:
B4b shipped only the close-mirror half of the originally scoped behavior (GH closed -> balls closed, gated by close_mirror policy). Deferred to this follow-up:

- GH title change mirrored back to balls task.title when it is safe (balls hasn't edited the title since last sync). Requires push half (B3) to also write last_synced_title to the projection so 'has balls moved' is decidable. Also requires balls core to accept title overwrite via SyncReport.updated.fields['title'] (verify in B6 integration test).
- GH body change mirrored back to balls task.description. Requires push half to write a content hash (last_synced_body_hash) bounded in size per SPEC 6 'no new state stores' intent.
- Conflict detection when both sides moved a field: emit a verbose add_note listing GH's view and balls's view, do NOT modify that field. SPEC 8's structured 'conflict' propose-response is push-side; the pull half conveys conflict via add_note since SyncReport has no conflict variant.
- last_synced_status update on PULL side too (currently only push writes it). Without this, a pull-mirror followed by another sync re-emits the same update. Today the second sync's updated_from sees task.status == 'closed' and returns None — works correctly but only by accident (the close branch checks task.status != closed first). Once title/body mirror is added, this assumption breaks; the projection must update on the pull side, which means SyncReport.updated.fields needs to set 'external.github_issues.issue.last_synced_*' OR balls core must support deeper merge semantics — verify in B6.

ACCEPTANCE: title/body mirror with conflict-aware add_notes; bounded last_synced_body hash; pull side updates projection so a second sync is a noop; integration test (B6) confirms the full echo-free roundtrip.
title: B4b extended: content mirror (title/body) + conflict-via-SyncReport for GH-side issue edits
